recommended_approach.choice: Option C - Embed dedicated DB upsert in sync_links.py
rationale: Self-contained: Calendly sync is a specialized integration with specific knowledge of event type structure
Atomic: Single transaction for both file and DB ensures consistency
Merge logic: Calendly-specific context inference (e.g., 'friends & family' → audience:personal) belongs in the sync script
Performance: No subprocess overhead from calling external scripts
Maintainability: All Calendly-related logic in one place

rejected_alternatives.option_a_content_ingest: content_ingest.py is designed for single-file ingestion from frontmatter, not API-driven bulk sync
rejected_alternatives.option_b_content_library_add: content_library.py add() has schema drift (uses 'type' column vs actual 'content_type'), and doesn't support non-destructive merge for tags

properties: Deterministic: same event always produces same ID
Collision-free: Calendly slugs are unique per user

behavior.curated: Only fill if missing/empty - never overwrite existing values
behavior.managed: Always update to current inferred value

merge_policy.implementation: merge_tags() function compares existing vs new defaults, returns only safe changes

key_functions.generate_stable_id(): Creates deterministic DB id from event type slug or UUID
key_functions.infer_context_defaults(): Conservative heuristics for audience/purpose from event name
key_functions.upsert_db_record(): INSERT or UPDATE with merge logic

cli_options: --db-only: Skip markdown file generation, only update DB

notes_for_orchestrator: SCHEMA ISSUE: content_library.py add() method uses INSERT with 'type' column but actual schema has 'content_type'. This will fail if anyone calls lib.add(). Should be fixed in W1.2.
LEGACY DATA: 7 Calendly items exist in DB from 'json_migration' source (vrijen_* IDs). These are separate from the new calendly_* IDs and may need migration/dedupe in W2.2.
RECOMMENDATION: Add 'subtype' tag for scheduling-link to align with taxonomy if W1.1 spec specifies subtypes.
SYNC TRIGGER: Consider adding Calendly sync to the daily scheduled task or webhook-based trigger.
